message:
The other day, I found myself rummaging through our old family board games while preparing our go-bags. It was a delightfully nostalgic trip down memory lane! I set up a game of Scrabble with my kids, and we ended up laughing so hard over silly words and friendly competition. It reminded me that sometimes, the best moments of connection happen without screens. 

In the spirit of being prepared, I realized it’s not just about having supplies ready, but also about keeping our family engaged and connected during any situation. Rediscovering low-tech fun can be a wonderful way to build those bonds. 

Have you had any fun moments like this lately? What’s your favorite family game?
